codeReview.summary: Reviewed commit d0a49513fcc2409c475609e0b335a5c2f03e6726, the worker handoff, the transcript skeleton, and the touched auth/company/admin tests. The filter hardening correctly preserves canonical `/api/v1/superadmin/**` access for root/platform-scoped superadmins, blocks tenant-attached superadmin access to tenant workflows, and retires several nested `/api/v1/companies/**` aliases to 404. However, the packet still leaves a second live cross-tenant inventory surface at `GET /api/v1/companies` for root-scoped superadmins, so the hard cut is incomplete and the feature does not fully satisfy the stated route-retirement/privacy-wall requirements.

issues.description: `GET /api/v1/companies` still returns `companyService.findAll()` for any `ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN`, which preserves a non-canonical cross-tenant tenant-inventory surface outside `/api/v1/superadmin/**`. The feature description and mission architecture require platform-owner inventory reads to live on canonical `/api/v1/superadmin/**` routes and retire nested `/api/v1/companies/**` control-plane aliases from runtime behavior. Because root-scoped superadmins can still list all tenants through `/api/v1/companies`, the privacy-wall hard cut is incomplete and there are still two runtime truths for tenant inventory.

The new regression `list_companies_returns_all_tenants_for_root_only_super_admin` locks in the surviving `/api/v1/companies` superadmin inventory behavior instead of retiring it. That test codifies the same non-canonical route surface the feature was supposed to remove, so the packet actively preserves the duplicate control-plane host rather than proving it fails closed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission architecture documents `/api/v1/superadmin/**` as the canonical platform control-plane host, but there is no shared-state note or guard explaining whether `GET /api/v1/companies` is an intentional exception. This packet preserves that route for root-scoped superadmins, creating runtime truth that conflicts with the documented canonical route matrix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:32-35 defines the platform control plane under `/api/v1/superadmin/**`, while erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/company/controller/CompanyController.java:27-35 still serves `GET /api/v1/companies` and erp-domain/src/test/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/company/CompanyControllerIT.java:111-123 now codifies superadmin-wide tenant listing on that host.

summary: Fail. The packet hardens the canonical superadmin filter paths correctly, but it does not complete the requested hard cut because root-scoped superadmins can still read cross-tenant inventory via `GET /api/v1/companies`. That surviving duplicate host conflicts with the mission’s canonical `/api/v1/superadmin/**` route ownership and keeps the control-plane alias retirement incomplete.
